rules: Investment horizon above 15 years increases risk capacity
Near-term goals under 5 years reduce ability to take equity risk
High loss aversion should cap aggressive portfolio recommendations
Use both emotional tolerance and financial capacity when assigning risk level
Emergency fund should remain highly liquid
Home purchase funds within 5 years should be in lower-volatility assets
Long-term retirement assets can be invested for growth
Do not mix short-term and long-term goals into one undifferentiated portfolio
Flag portfolios heavily concentrated in one stock or sector
Employer stock concentration should be treated as a risk factor
Diversification across asset classes and sectors reduces idiosyncratic risk
Recommendations must align with the stated risk profile
Time horizon and liquidity needs must be incorporated into advice
